skill.name: azure-storage-blob-java
skill.description: Build blob storage applications with Azure Storage Blob SDK for Java. Use when uploading, downloading, or managing files in Azure Blob Storage, working with containers, or implementing streaming data operations.
skill.summary: Azure Blob Storage SDK for Java documentation covering upload, download, container management, SAS tokens, and streaming operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is pure documentation for Azure Storage Blob SDK for Java. Static analyzer detected 49 'external_commands' patterns and 1 'network' pattern, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The backticks are markdown code block delimiters (```java, ```xml, ```bash), not shell command execution. The URL is a documentation example for environment variable configuration. No executable code, no command injection vectors, no malicious patterns detected.

risk_factor_evidence.reasoning: Static analyzer misidentified markdown code block backticks as shell command execution. All detections are within documentation code examples, not actual runtime command execution.
The detected URL at line 380 is a documentation example showing how to set AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_URL environment variable. This is not actual network code execution.

content.user_title: Manage Azure Blob Storage with Java SDK
content.value_statement: Store and retrieve unstructured data efficiently using Azure Blob Storage. This skill provides complete Java SDK patterns for uploading, downloading, and managing blobs with proper authentication and error handling.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage blob containers with various authentication methods
Upload blobs from strings, files, or streams with custom headers and metadata
Download blobs to files, streams, or in-memory data structures
Generate SAS tokens for secure time-limited access
List blobs with hierarchy support and metadata retrieval
Handle blob leases, copies, and lifecycle operations
limitations: Requires valid Azure storage account and credentials
SDK version 12.33.0 or compatible required for all patterns
Some operations require network connectivity to Azure endpoints
Large file operations may need parallel upload configuration

use_cases.title: Cloud Application Developer
use_cases.description: Build scalable applications that store user uploads, logs, or media files in Azure Blob Storage with proper authentication using DefaultAzureCredential or connection strings.

use_cases.title: Data Engineer
use_cases.description: Implement data lake patterns with hierarchical blob listing, metadata management, and streaming operations for large datasets.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers working with Azure data platforms

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer
use_cases.description: Automate backup and archival workflows with SAS token generation, blob copy operations, and lifecycle management.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Blob Upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to upload a text file to Azure Blob Storage using Java SDK with a connection string.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Simple file upload to a blob container

prompt_templates.title: Container Management
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a blob container if it does not exist, then list all blobs with a specific prefix and download them to a local folder.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Container operations with conditional creation and batch download

prompt_templates.title: SAS Token Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a SAS token for a blob that allows read-only access for 24 hours, then show how to use it to download the blob without credentials.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Secure shared access with time-limited tokens

prompt_templates.title: Streaming Large Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload a large file using parallel upload with custom content type and metadata, handle retries on failure, and set appropriate HTTP headers for caching.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Production-ready streaming upload with error handling and optimization

output_examples.input: Upload a JSON configuration file to a container named 'configs' with blob name 'app-settings.json'
output: Uploaded app-settings.json (2.4 KB) with content-type: application/json

output_examples.input: List all blobs in 'data/2024/' prefix and download each to local ./downloads folder

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for production deployments to avoid hardcoded credentials
Set appropriate content-type and cache-control headers when uploading blobs
Use SAS tokens with minimal permissions and short expiry times for shared access
Implement retry policies and handle BlobStorageException for transient failures
Use parallel upload options for files larger than 100 MB
anti_patterns: Never commit connection strings or SAS tokens to source control
Avoid using account keys in application code - prefer managed identities or SAS tokens
Do not set SAS token expiry times longer than necessary for the use case
Avoid downloading entire containers without pagination for large datasets

faq.question: What authentication methods does Azure Blob Storage SDK support?
faq.answer: The SDK supports connection strings, Shared Access Signatures (SAS), Azure Active Directory credentials via DefaultAzureCredential, and account keys. For production, use DefaultAzureCredential with managed identities or service principals.

faq.question: How do I handle large file uploads efficiently?
faq.answer: Use BlobParallelUploadOptions for files larger than 100 MB. The SDK automatically chunks the file and uploads in parallel. Configure chunk size and concurrency based on your network capacity.

faq.question: What is the difference between BlobClient, BlobContainerClient, and BlobServiceClient?
faq.answer: BlobServiceClient manages the storage account level operations. BlobContainerClient manages a specific container within the account. BlobClient operates on a specific blob within a container.

faq.question: How do I securely share blob access with external users?
faq.answer: Generate SAS tokens with specific permissions (read, write, etc.) and expiry times. Use container-level SAS for multiple blobs or blob-level SAS for single file access. Never share account keys.

faq.question: Can I organize blobs in folders within a container?
faq.answer: Azure Blob Storage uses a flat namespace but supports hierarchical naming with forward slashes in blob names. Use the prefix parameter in listBlobs operations to simulate folder navigation.

faq.question: How do I handle blob storage errors and retries?
faq.answer: Catch BlobStorageException to handle specific error codes (404 for not found, 409 for conflicts). The SDK includes built-in retry policies. Configure retry options in client builder for production resilience.
